title: hieracium pilosella subsp. subvulgare
intro: Hieracium pilosella subsp. subvulgare, commonly known as a subspecies of the Hawkweed plant, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The medicinal properties of Hieracium pilosella subsp. subvulgare have been found to help with respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions like eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of this plant are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to alleviate these health issues.
